_comment: Example MCP configuration for Claude Code. Copy to mcp.json and fill in values.

databricks.description: Query Unity Catalog, run SQL, access MLflow experiments

github.description: Create PRs, manage repos, track issues

brave-search.description: Search for ML best practices and research

context7.description: Get up-to-date library documentation (XGBoost, MLflow, etc.)

databricks: 1. Install Databricks CLI: pip install databricks-cli
3. The managed MCP uses your Databricks CLI auth automatically
github: 1. Create a Personal Access Token (PAT) in GitHub Settings
2. Grant: repo (Full control of private repositories)
obsidian: 2. Set OBSIDIAN_VAULT to your vault path
3. Enable Local REST API in Obsidian settings

databricks: List tables: 'Show me all tables in hackathon_catalog.grocery'
Query data: 'What's the distribution of reorder rates by department?'
Check experiments: 'List my recent MLflow experiments'
github: Create PR: 'Create a pull request with my experiment results'
Create issue: 'Create an issue for feature engineering improvements'
obsidian: Document: 'Create a note documenting my best experiment'
Search: 'Find my notes about hyperparameter tuning'
brave-search: Research: 'Search for XGBoost early stopping best practices'
Find papers: 'Find recent papers on demand forecasting'
context7: Docs: 'Using context7, show me MLflow autolog documentation'
Examples: 'What's the XGBoost syntax for custom objectives?'
memory: Recall: 'What hyperparameters worked best in our experiments?'
